intro: Alsophila rouhaniana, also known as the Tahiti tree fern, is a species of plant in the family Cyatheaceae, native to French Polynesia. The plant has been traditionally used in Polynesian medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and digestive problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties.
